question: Write a C++ program that declares an array of 5 integers and initializes it. Then, ask the user to enter a number to search for. If the number is found in the array, print its index (position); otherwise, print a 'not found' message.

test_cases.expected:
Attempting to access arr[5], which is out of bounds.
not found

errors: The loop condition 'i <= arr.size()' allows the loop to iterate one element too far, attempting to access 'arr[arr.size()]' (i.e., 'arr[5]' for a 5-element array). This is an out-of-bounds access, leading to undefined behavior. While the added 'if (i == arr.size())' check prevents a crash, it still highlights the intent to access an invalid index.
